title: Caprese Salad
tagline: Tomato, mozzarella, basil. The holy trinity.
description: The simplest Italian salad - ripe tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, basil, olive oil, and nothing else. Quality ingredients are everything here.
ingredients: 1 lb fresh mozzarella (not the shredded kind)
steps: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a platter, slightly overlapping.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices.
Season with flaky salt and cracked pepper.
If using, drizzle balsamic glaze in a zigzag pattern.
notes: Use room temperature tomatoes - cold kills the flavor.
Fresh mozzarella (the soft kind in water) is non-negotiable.
This is a summer dish. Don't bother with winter tomatoes.
testedNote: Verified as written. The only failure mode is bad tomatoes.
troubleshooting: Tastes like nothing → cold or out-of-season tomatoes; room temperature, peak summer, or make something else.
Watery platter → mozzarella not drained; let slices sit on paper towels 10 minutes.
Balsamic drowned it → glaze is optional for a reason; a zigzag, not a pour.
